description_long: This object consists of two rectangular advertising boards, each with a solid, boxy structure and flat faces. The boards are made primarily of wood or a wood-like material, with visible grain and a matte finish on the sides and back. The front faces display printed advertisements: one features a vintage-style illustration with Cyrillic text and a cityscape, while the other shows two figures and a building, also with Cyrillic text. The edges are slightly worn, suggesting some age or outdoor exposure. Both boards are freestanding and appear to be designed for temporary or portable placement in an urban environment.
description: Two freestanding rectangular advertising boards with printed vintage-style graphics and wooden frames, showing signs of wear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness and boxy structure of both boards, with one board's printed face partially visible.
description_view_1: Front view displaying both advertising faces: one with a cityscape and Cyrillic text, the other with two figures and a building.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the depth and wood grain texture of the boards, with the edge of one printed face visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the plain wooden backs of both boards, with no visible graphics or text.
attribute_frame_design: thick wooden border with squared edges
attribute_advertisement_content: static printed posters with vintage-style graphics and Cyrillic text
